original: Even in traditional offices, “the lingua franca of corporate America has gotten much more emotional and much more right-brained than it was 20 years ago,” said Harvard Business School professor Nancy Koehn.
translation_literal: 哈佛商学院教授南希·科恩说，即使在传统办公室里，“美国企业界的通用语也已经变得比20年前情绪化得多、更加右脑化。”

chunks.english: the lingua franca of corporate America has gotten much more emotional and much more right-brained than it was 20 years ago
chunks.chinese: 美国企业界的通用语已经变得比20年前情绪化得多、更加右脑化

chunks.english: Harvard Business School professor Nancy Koehn
chunks.chinese: 哈佛商学院教授南希·科恩
chunks.relation: 倒装后的主语

chunks.english: the lingua franca of corporate America
chunks.relation: 宾语从句「the lingua franca of corporate America has gotten much more emotional and much more right-brained than it was 20 years ago」中的主语部分
宾语从句「the lingua franca of corporate America has gotten much more emotional and much more right-brained than it was 20 years ago」中的谓语部分

宾语从句「the lingua franca of corporate America has gotten much more emotional and much more right-brained than it was 20 years ago」中的表语部分，作用于「the lingua franca of corporate America has gotten much more emotional and much more right-brained than it was 20 years ago」

宾语从句「the lingua franca of corporate America has gotten much more emotional and much more right-brained than it was 20 years ago」中的表语部分，作用于「the lingua franca of corporate America has gotten much more emotional and much more right-brained than it was 20 years ago」

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 直接引语 + said + 主语
sentence_patterns.explanation: 直接引语置前，said 后接主语，阅读时可先识别说话者。

chunks.relation: 作 spinning off 的宾语

original: “If you and I parachuted back to Fortune 500 companies in 1990, we would see much less frequent use of terms like journey, mission, passion.”
translation_literal: “如果你和我空降回1990年的《财富》500强公司，我们会看到像 journey、mission、passion 这样的词使用频率少得多。”

chunks.english: If you and I parachuted back to Fortune 500 companies in 1990
chunks.chinese: 如果你和我空降回1990年的《财富》500强公司

chunks.english: much less frequent use of terms like journey, mission, passion
chunks.chinese: 像 journey、mission、passion 这样的词使用频率少得多
chunks.relation: 作 would see 的宾语
if 条件状语从句内部主语
if 条件状语从句内部谓语

original: There were goals, there were strategies, there were objectives, but we didn’t talk about energy; we didn’t talk about passion.
translation_literal: 那时有目标，有策略，有目的，但是我们不谈能量；我们不谈激情。

chunks.english: There were goals, there were strategies, there were objectives
but we didn’t talk about energy
chunks.relation: 与前面形成转折
与前一分句并列
并列分句「There were goals, there were strategies, there were objectives」中的并列部分
并列分句「There were goals, there were strategies, there were objectives」中的并列部分
并列分句「There were goals, there were strategies, there were objectives」中的并列部分
there be 并列分句中的形式主语
there be 并列分句中的谓语

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: there were A, there were B, there were C; we did not...
sentence_patterns.explanation: 多个 there be 结构与后面两个否定分句并列，突出新旧企业语言差异。

original: Koehn pointed out that this new era of corporate vocabulary is very “team”-oriented—and not by coincidence.
translation_literal: 科恩指出，企业词汇的这个新时代非常以“团队”为导向，而且这并非巧合。

chunks.english: that this new era of corporate vocabulary is very team-oriented
chunks.chinese: 企业词汇的这个新时代非常以团队为导向
chunks.relation: 作 pointed out 的宾语
补充说明这种导向不是偶然

chunks.english: this new era of corporate vocabulary
chunks.relation: 宾语从句内部主语
宾语从句内部谓语
宾语从句内部表语

sentence_patterns.explanation: and not by coincidence 是破折号后的补充判断。

original: “Let’s not forget sports—in male-dominated corporate America, it’s still a big deal.”
translation_literal: “我们不要忘记体育——在男性主导的美国企业界，它仍然是一件大事。”

chunks.english: in male-dominated corporate America, it’s still a big deal
chunks.chinese: 在男性主导的美国企业界，它仍然是一件大事
chunks.relation: 破折号后解释 sports 的重要性

sentence_patterns.explanation: 破折号后说明 sports 为什么与企业语言相关。

original: “It’s not explicitly conscious; it’s the idea that I’m a coach, and you’re my team, and we’re in this together.”
translation_literal: “这并不是明确有意识的；这是一种观念，即我是教练，你是我的团队，我们一起参与其中。”

chunks.chinese: 这并不是明确有意识的

chunks.relation: 与前一分句并列

chunks.english: that I’m a coach, and you’re my team, and we’re in this together
chunks.chinese: 即我是教练，你是我的团队，我们一起参与其中
chunks.relation: 解释 the idea 的内容
同位语从句「that I’m a coach, and you’re my team, and we’re in this together」内部主语
同位语从句「that I’m a coach, and you’re my team, and we’re in this together」内部谓语
同位语从句「that I’m a coach, and you’re my team, and we’re in this together」内部表语，作用于「that I’m a coach, and you’re my team, and we’re in this together」
同位语从句「that I’m a coach, and you’re my team, and we’re in this together」中的并列部分
同位语从句「that I’m a coach, and you’re my team, and we’re in this together」中的并列部分

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 抽象名词 + that 从句
sentence_patterns.explanation: that 从句解释 the idea 的具体内容。
that 从句内部三个分句并列，构成企业团队隐喻。

original: There are lots and lots of CEOs in very different companies, but most think of themselves as coaches and this is their team and they want to win.
translation_literal: 在非常不同的公司里有许许多多的首席执行官，但大多数人把自己看作教练，而且这是他们的团队，他们想要获胜。

chunks.english: There are lots and lots of CEOs in very different companies
chunks.chinese: 在非常不同的公司里有许许多多的首席执行官

chunks.english: but most think of themselves as coaches
chunks.chinese: 但大多数人把自己看作教练
chunks.relation: 与前面形成转折
与前一分句并列
继续并列
there be 分句中的真实主语
there be 分句中的谓语
说明 CEOs 所在范围

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: but A and B and C
sentence_patterns.explanation: but 后连续三个分句并列，说明 CEOs 的共同思维。

original: These terms are also intended to infuse work with meaning—and, as Khurana, another professor, points out, increase allegiance to the firm.
translation_literal: 这些词语还意在给工作注入意义，并且，正如另一位教授库拉纳指出的那样，增强对公司的忠诚。

chunks.relation: intended 后第一个不定式目的内容

chunks.english: and increase allegiance to the firm
chunks.relation: 与 to infuse... 并列，表示第二个目的内容

chunks.english: as Khurana, another professor, points out
chunks.chinese: 正如另一位教授库拉纳指出的那样
chunks.relation: 插入说明观点来源
解释 Khurana 身份
第二个并列不定式中的动作核心
插入语「as Khurana, another professor, points out」内部主语
插入语「as Khurana, another professor, points out」内部谓语

sentence_patterns.explanation: as Khurana...points out 是插入式 as 结构，表示“正如……指出”。

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 并列结构中插入观点来源
sentence_patterns.explanation: as Khurana...points out 插入在 and 与 increase 之间，阅读时可先跳过。

original: “You have the importation of terminology that historically used to be associated with non-profit organizations and religious organizations: terms like vision, values, passion, and purpose,” said Khurana.
translation_literal: 库拉纳说：“你会看到术语的引入，这些术语历史上曾经与非营利组织和宗教组织相关：比如 vision、values、passion 和 purpose 这样的词。”

chunks.relation: 作 have 的宾语

chunks.english: that historically used to be associated with non-profit organizations and religious organizations
chunks.chinese: 历史上曾经与非营利组织和宗教组织相关的
chunks.relation: 修饰 terminology

chunks.english: terms like vision, values, passion, and purpose
chunks.chinese: 比如 vision、values、passion 和 purpose 这样的词
chunks.relation: 冒号后举例解释 terminology
说明引语来源
定语从句内部主语，指 terminology
定语从句内部谓语

chunks.chinese: 非营利组织和宗教组织
chunks.relation: 作 associated with 的宾语

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 冒号后举例解释前文名词
sentence_patterns.explanation: 冒号后的 terms like... 解释 terminology。

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 直接引语 + said + 主语
sentence_patterns.explanation: said Khurana 位于引语后，是引语来源倒装。

original: This new focus on personal fulfillment can help keep employees motivated amid increasingly loud debates over work-life balance.
translation_literal: 这种对个人成就感的新关注能够帮助员工在关于工作与生活平衡的日益激烈的争论中保持积极性。

chunks.english: This new focus on personal fulfillment
chunks.chinese: 这种对个人成就感的新关注

chunks.relation: 作 keep 的复合宾语

chunks.english: amid increasingly loud debates over work-life balance
chunks.chinese: 在关于工作与生活平衡的日益激烈的争论中
chunks.relation: 说明背景

original: The “mommy wars” of the 1990s are still going on today, prompting arguments about why women still can’t have it all and books like Sheryl Sandberg’s Lean In, whose title has become a buzzword in its own right.
translation_literal: 20世纪90年代的“妈妈战争”今天仍在继续，引发了关于为什么女性仍然不能拥有一切的争论，以及像谢丽尔·桑德伯格的《向前一步》这样的书，该书标题本身已经成为一个流行词。

chunks.relation: 说明时间

chunks.english: prompting arguments about why women still can’t have it all and books like Sheryl Sandberg’s Lean In
chunks.chinese: 引发关于女性为什么仍不能拥有一切的争论以及像《向前一步》这样的书
chunks.relation: 说明结果

chunks.english: whose title has become a buzzword in its own right
chunks.chinese: 其标题本身已经成为一个流行词
chunks.relation: 修饰 Lean In
whose 定语从句内部主语
whose 定语从句内部谓语

chunks.english: a buzzword in its own right
chunks.relation: whose 定语从句内部表语

sentence_patterns.pattern_name: 分裂结构 / 间隔结构
sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 现在分词短语带长宾语
sentence_patterns.explanation: prompting 后的宾语很长，包含 arguments 和 books 两部分，容易误读。

key_phrases.meaning: 拥有一切，事业家庭兼得

original: Terms like unplug, offline, life-hack, bandwidth, and capacity are all about setting boundaries between the office and the home.
translation_literal: 像 unplug、offline、life-hack、bandwidth 和 capacity 这样的词都关乎在办公室和家庭之间设定界限。

chunks.english: Terms like unplug, offline, life-hack, bandwidth, and capacity
chunks.chinese: 像 unplug、offline、life-hack、bandwidth 和 capacity 这样的词

chunks.english: all about setting boundaries between the office and the home
chunks.chinese: 都关乎在办公室和家庭之间设定界限
chunks.relation: 作 are 的表语

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: A, B, C, D, and E
sentence_patterns.explanation: 多个术语并列作主语核心内容，统一指向 setting boundaries。

original: But if your work is your “passion,” you’ll be more likely to devote yourself to it, even if that means going home for dinner and then working long after the kids are in bed.
translation_literal: 但是，如果你的工作就是你的“激情”，你就更可能投身于它，即使那意味着回家吃晚饭，然后在孩子们上床后很久还继续工作。

chunks.chinese: 如果你的工作就是你的“激情”

chunks.english: more likely to devote yourself to it
chunks.relation: 作 will be 的表语

chunks.english: even if that means going home for dinner and then working long after the kids are in bed
chunks.chinese: 即使那意味着回家吃晚饭，然后在孩子们上床后很久还继续工作
chunks.relation: 说明让步情况
even if 让步状语从句内部主语
even if 让步状语从句内部谓语

chunks.english: going home for dinner and then working long after the kids are in bed
chunks.chinese: 回家吃晚饭然后在孩子睡后继续工作很久
chunks.relation: 作 means 的宾语

original: But this seems to be the irony of office speak: Everyone makes fun of it, but managers love it, companies depend on it, and regular people willingly absorb it.
translation_literal: 但这似乎是办公室语言的讽刺之处：每个人都取笑它，但管理者喜欢它，公司依赖它，普通人也心甘情愿地吸收它。

chunks.chinese: 办公室语言的讽刺之处
chunks.relation: 作 seems to be 的表语

chunks.english: Everyone makes fun of it, but managers love it, companies depend on it, and regular people willingly absorb it
chunks.chinese: 每个人都取笑它，但管理者喜欢它，公司依赖它，普通人也心甘情愿地吸收它
chunks.relation: 冒号后解释 irony 的具体内容

sentence_patterns.explanation: 冒号后的多个分句解释 the irony of office speak。

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: A, but B, C, and D
sentence_patterns.explanation: 冒号后多个分句并列，形成“嘲笑但接受”的讽刺。

original: As linguist once said, “You can get people to think it’s nonsense at the same time that you buy into it.”
translation_literal: 正如一位语言学家曾经说的：“你可以让人们在接受它的同时认为它是废话。”

chunks.chinese: 正如一位语言学家曾经说的

chunks.relation: 作 get 的宾语

chunks.english: to think it’s nonsense at the same time that you buy into it
chunks.chinese: 在接受它的同时认为它是废话
chunks.relation: 补充说明 people 的动作

sentence_patterns.explanation: As linguist once said 是插入式 as 结构，表示引用依据。

original: In a workplace that’s fundamentally indifferent to your life and its meaning, office speak can help you figure out how you relate to your work—and how your work defines who you are.
translation_literal: 在一个从根本上对你的生活及其意义漠不关心的工作场所中，办公室语言可以帮助你弄清你如何与工作发生关系，以及你的工作如何定义你是谁。
